nativeRange.habitat: Damp banks and shallow margins of streams and seasonally flooded freshwater habitats in subtropical South America, often growing emersed on wet substrate and becoming submerged when water levels rise

decorPreferences: fine, smooth substrate to anchor delicate roots
open foreground with strong direct light
pressurised CO2 injection beneficial but not strictly required
stable water column with regular fertilisation

avoidWith: boisterous fish that uproot fine carpet plants

summary: Monte Carlo is a bright-green South American carpeting plant (Micranthemum tweediei) valued for its small, rounded leaves on creeping stems. Widely regarded as easier to grow than HC Cuba, it spreads into a soft foreground carpet under medium-to-high light and benefits strongly from CO2 supplementation.
careNotes: Monte Carlo (Micranthemum tweediei), occasionally sold under the names 'New Large Pearl Grass' or, incorrectly, as various Bacopa cultivars, is a South American creeping herb in the family Linderniaceae, native to Argentina and Uruguay. It rose to prominence in the aquascaping hobby in the early 2010s as a less demanding substitute for Hemianthus callitrichoides 'HC Cuba': the leaves are noticeably larger (typically 3–6 mm) and the plant produces a more vigorous, forgiving carpet that anchors and spreads more readily. Light demand is moderate to high — under low light the plant tends to grow leggy and reach upward instead of carpeting horizontally, so strong direct lighting over the foreground is recommended. CO2 is not strictly required but is strongly recommended for tight horizontal carpets and faster, denser growth; without it the plant generally survives but spreads slowly and can lose its compact form. Temperature tolerance is broad, generally 20–28 °C, with pH 6.0–7.5 and gH 3–15 well tolerated. A fine, nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil with iron-rich enrichment) supports rapid rooting, and regular dosing of macro- and micronutrients to the water column helps keep growth dense. Plant by dividing tissue-culture or potted portions into small plugs of a few stems each and spacing them 2–3 cm apart across the foreground. Once established, trim every 2–3 weeks to keep the lower layer illuminated and prevent the mat from lifting. The plant is commonly mislabelled in the trade as 'Bacopa monnieri Monte Carlo' or 'Bacopa sp. Monte Carlo', neither of which reflects its correct genus placement.
breedingNotes: Propagation is vegetative: the carpet spreads horizontally by creeping stems that root at the nodes, so aquascapers expand coverage by lifting a mature mat, dividing it into small clumps of a few stems each, and replanting them a few centimetres apart across the foreground. Trimmed cuttings with one or two nodes can be replanted to fill bare spots and will root quickly under good conditions. A dry start method (growing emersed on damp substrate under a sealed lid for 3–6 weeks before flooding) is widely used to establish a dense, well-rooted carpet before submerged conditions begin.

primary.name: Aquarium Co-Op
primary.notes: Aquarium Co-Op product page for Monte Carlo (Micranthemum sp. 'Monte Carlo'). Confirmed medium-to-high light, carpeting growth habit with lateral creeping along substrate, low-maintenance care, bright-green coloration, suitability as a soft carpet for shrimp and bottom dwellers, and foreground placement.

additional.name: Modern Aquarium
additional.notes: Confirmed scientific name Micranthemum tweediei, strong lighting requirement for compact carpet, CO2 'not required but strongly recommended for faster, denser carpeting', moderate-to-fast growth rate with CO2, foreground/low-midground placement, propagation by dividing into plugs and trimming, nutrient-rich substrate recommendation, temperature 68–90 °F (20–32 °C; conservative upper bound averaged with other sources), and easy-to-moderate care level.

additional.name: Aquarium Plants Factory (Tropica 025)
additional.notes: Tropica item 025 / 025 TC listing carried by Aquarium Plants Factory. Confirmed scientific name Micranthemum tweediei 'Monte Carlo', carpeting plant type, Tropica difficulty 'Medium', moderate-to-high light demand, CO2 benefits supplementation, moderate growth rate, foreground placement, South America origin, and the explicit framing that it is 'much easier to grow than Micranthemum callitrichoides Cuba'. Used in lieu of Tropica's own plantdetails page which returned an XSLT parsing error on direct fetch. The Buce Plant URL supplied in the task brief (https://buceplant.com/products/monte-carlo) returned HTTP 404 and was not used.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Micranthemum
additional.notes: Confirmed genus Micranthemum belongs to family Linderniaceae (order Lamiales), distribution Eastern USA to Tropical and Subtropical America, and that Micranthemum tweediei is one of the recognised species in the genus. Used as the taxonomic anchor for family/order fields.
